info.text: In this demo I’ve imported Tone.js, a library created by musician and instrument maker Yotam Mann (with help from other folks) that turns the Web Audio API into musician-friendly building blocks.
info.title: getting started
Web Audio API
info.text: Instead of wiring low-level nodes by hand, Tone.js lets you work with instruments, notes, timing, and effects, perfect for sketches where you want sound quickly without losing control. This demo might seem simple, but it would require a lot more code had we written it directly with the Web Audio API.

info.title: global var
info.text: First, we'll declare a global variable synth so we can access our Tone.js instrument across different functions.

info.title: setup
info.text: Then, as with many of our other demos, we'll define a setup function. This is where we'll create a synthesizer instrument with new Tone.Synth(). Calling .toDestination() routes the synth’s output to your speakers (the audio context’s destination). Under the hood, Tone creates an oscillator and envelope and connects them in the audio graph so it’s ready to play.

info.title: play
info.text: The play function performs one note with .triggerAttackRelease('C4', '8n'). The pitch is given in this notation, where 'C4' is middle C (the 4th C key on a piano), and the duration uses musical time '8n', or an "eighth note". Tone converts those musical values into the correct frequency and milliseconds based on the current tempo, so your timing stays musical if you change BPM later.

info.title: load and click
info.text: We attach two listeners: on load we run setup to create and route the synth, and on click we call play. Modern browsers require a user gesture before starting audio, connecting playback to a click both satisfies that policy and makes the demo interactive, click anywhere on the rendered page and you’ll hear a short tone.

info.title: lots more
info.text: There’s loads more you can do with this library, this is only the beginning. Tone.js has excellent docs and examples. Check out Nick's AlgorithmicMusic.Online course for a much deeper dive.
